The following article gives a list of [association football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") confederations, sub-confederations and associations around the world. The sport's international governing body is [FIFA](/wiki/FIFA "FIFA"), but those associations not affiliated with FIFA are also included in this article.

Most European, African, and Asian countries have two principal competitions: a more prestigious league which is typically a double [round-robin tournament](/wiki/Round-robin_tournament "Round-robin tournament") restricted to the elite clubs, and a cup which is a [single-elimination tournament](/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament "Single-elimination tournament") open to both the elite and lesser clubs.

In the Americas, leagues are often organised as either [multi-stage tournaments](/wiki/Tournament#Multi-stage_tournaments "Tournament") or separate [Apertura and Clausura](/wiki/Apertura_and_Clausura "Apertura and Clausura") stages.

## Non-FIFA associations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geography_of_association_football&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Non-FIFA associations")]

See also: [List of non-national representative teams in men's football](/wiki/List_of_non-national_representative_teams_in_men%27s_football "List of non-national representative teams in men's football") and [List of top-division football clubs in non-FIFA countries](/wiki/List_of_top-division_football_clubs_in_non-FIFA_countries "List of top-division football clubs in non-FIFA countries")

The football associations in this section represent fully and partially recognized [sovereign states](/wiki/Sovereign_state "Sovereign state"), [dependent territories](/wiki/Dependent_territory "Dependent territory"), and [stateless nations](/wiki/Stateless_nation "Stateless nation") that are not members of FIFA or a FIFA confederation.

Several of these non-FIFA associations are included in the [World Football Elo Ratings](/wiki/World_Football_Elo_Ratings "World Football Elo Ratings"), which ranks all national association football teams from FIFA-affiliated confederations, in addition to some unaffiliated teams. Several of these associations are also members of the [Confederation of Independent Football Associations](/wiki/Confederation_of_Independent_Football_Associations "Confederation of Independent Football Associations") and/or the [International Island Games Association](/wiki/International_Island_Games_Association "International Island Games Association").

### Regional

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Geography_of_association_football&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Regional")]

Beneath the national level, governance of football may be divided up into regional or territorial associations. Other non-national associations represent stateless populations, [diasporas](/wiki/Diaspora "Diaspora") or [micronations](/wiki/Micronation "Micronation"). Details of these are listed at [non-FIFA international football](/wiki/Non-FIFA_international_football "Non-FIFA international football").
